question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that streamFileProvider.get(sidecarFilename) in getSidecar() fetches the sidecar from the same provider used for main record files — which may be a shared cloud bucket — to upload a crafted sidecar file at the expected path before the legitimate one is written, causing the importer to process the attacker's sidecar and authorize transactions without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a timeout or retry limit in the getSidecar() Mono pipeline to cause a denial-of-service that delays sidecar processing, and then substitute a forged sidecar at the expected path during the delay window, causing the importer to authorize transactions based on attacker-controlled sidecar data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a sidecar file with a consensusTimestamp that does not match any existing RecordItem, causing downloadSidecars() to silently discard the sidecar records without error, and then replay the same sidecar with a corrected timestamp in a subsequent download cycle to associate unauthorized records with a target transaction without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the .block() call in downloadSidecars() — which blocks the calling thread until all sidecars are processed — to inject a slow or infinite sidecar response that starves the download thread, preventing legitimate record files from being processed and creating a window for attacker-controlled files to be authorized without signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile with an empty getSidecars() list to skip downloadSidecars() entirely, then rely on the base class onVerified() to call streamFileNotifier.verified() with a RecordFile that has no sidecar verification, effectively authorizing transactions whose sidecar-dependent state changes were never validated against node signatures?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the cutoverService.getLastRecordFile().ifPresent() call in download() to inject a RecordFile with a manipulated hash field into the lastStreamFile AtomicReference, causing verifyHashChain() in the base Downloader to accept a forged subsequent file as the next valid chain link and authorize its transactions without signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a race condition between the cutoverService.get() callback and the scheduled download() invocation to cause lastStreamFile to be set to an empty Optional, triggering verifyHashChain() to log a warning and return true (bypassing hash chain verification) and authorizing transactions in the injected file without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Utility.archiveFile() call in getSidecar() — triggered when downloaderProperties.isWriteFiles() is true — to write attacker-controlled sidecar bytes to the archive destination, and then have those bytes re-read in a subsequent import cycle, authorizing transactions based on previously-injected unauthorized sidecar data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sidecar.setBytes(null) call in getSidecar() — triggered when sidecarProperties.isPersistBytes() is false — to cause the sidecar bytes to be cleared after hash verification but before the records are fully processed, creating a TOCTOU window where the sidecar content is replaced with attacker-controlled data and unauthorized transactions are authorized?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the flatMapIterable(SidecarFile::getRecords) step in downloadSidecars() to inject a SidecarFile whose getRecords() returns an attacker-controlled list of TransactionSidecarRecord objects that were never part of the original node-signed record stream, authorizing transactions without approval from the original signers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Multimaps.toMultimap() collection step in downloadSidecars() — which groups sidecar records by consensusTimestamp — to inject multiple sidecar records with the same timestamp as a legitimate transaction, causing recordItem.setSidecarRecords() to associate all of them (including attacker-crafted ones) with the transaction without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of signature verification on individual TransactionSidecarRecord entries in downloadSidecars() — the only check being the file-level hash in getSidecar() — to modify a sidecar file's records after the hash is computed but before it is fetched, authorizing transactions with tampered sidecar data without signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getSidecar() calls sidecarFileReader.read(sidecar, streamFileData) before performing the Arrays.equals(sidecar.getHash(), sidecar.getActualHash()) check, allowing a malicious sidecar payload to execute deserialization side effects that inject unauthorized transaction records into the sidecar object before the hash check can reject it?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the acceptedTypes.isEmpty() condition in the second filter of downloadSidecars() — which passes all records when no types are configured — to inject sidecar records of any type into the transaction items when the operator has not restricted sidecar types, authorizing transactions with unverified sidecar data without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between cutoverService.get() and the lastStreamFile AtomicReference to cause a scenario where the hash of the last stream file is null or empty, triggering the SHA_384.isHashEmpty() check in verifyHashChain() to return true and bypass hash chain verification, authorizing a forged record file's transactions without signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile where getSidecars() returns a list containing a SidecarFile with a name that, when passed to StreamFilename.from(), throws an InvalidStreamFileException, causing the Flux pipeline in downloadSidecars() to silently swallow the error and proceed with partially-processed sidecar data that authorizes transactions without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the streamFileProvider.get(sidecarFilename) call in getSidecar() — which uses the same provider as the main record file — to perform a path traversal attack via a crafted sidecarFilename, fetching a sidecar file from an unintended location and associating its unauthorized transaction records with legitimate items without signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that downloadSidecars() is called inside onVerified() — after the main record file has already passed signature verification — to inject a sidecar file that adds unauthorized contract state changes to already-verified transactions, effectively authorizing state transitions not approved by the original transaction signers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ordering of operations in onVerified() — where downloadSidecars() is called before super.onVerified() — to inject a sidecar that modifies RecordItem sidecarRecords, and then have those modified items passed to streamFileNotifier.verified() as if they were part of the originally-signed record file, authorizing transactions without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of a maximum sidecar count check in downloadSidecars() to inject a RecordFile with an extremely large getSidecars() list, causing the Flux pipeline to process thousands of attacker-controlled sidecar files and associate their unauthorized transaction records with legitimate items without signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a node-count consensus check for sidecar files in getSidecar() — unlike the main record file which requires 1/3 node stake consensus — to inject a sidecar file that is only present on one node's storage path, causing the importer to authorize transactions based on a single-node sidecar without multi-signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TransactionSidecarRecord::getConsensusTimestamp key extractor in the Multimaps.toMultimap() collector to inject a sidecar record with a timestamp that matches a high-value contract transaction, causing the importer to associate attacker-crafted contract state changes with that transaction and authorize unauthorized state without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between the @Scheduled annotation's fixedDelayString and the cutoverService.get() call to trigger multiple concurrent download() invocations, causing a race condition in lastStreamFile.set() that allows a forged RecordFile to be accepted as the last valid file and its transactions to be authorized without signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the sidecar hash stored in the RecordFile (sidecar.getHash()) is derived from the node-signed record file, but the actual sidecar bytes are fetched independently from the stream provider, to perform a substitution attack where the hash matches but the content has been replaced, authorizing transactions with tampered sidecar data without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the filter condition sidecar.getTypes().stream().anyMatch(acceptedTypes::contains) in downloadSidecars() by crafting a SidecarFile whose getTypes() list contains both a legitimate accepted type and an attacker-controlled type, causing the filter to pass and the attacker's unauthorized sidecar records to be associated with transaction items without signer approval?
